name: National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics
theoryOfChange: By uniting, supporting, and training Muslim charitable clinics, the association enhances their capability to provide high-quality care and expand services to underserved populations.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Limited match due to weaker results and/or higher uncertainty; review methodology details before deciding.

amalEvaluation.charity_name: National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics

components.evidence: Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 23/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, global health cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish current reserve levels and a board-approved reserve policy (often 3-12 months for most nonprofits).
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

components.evidence: Board governance: baseline (2 members, emerging org)
components.name: Governance

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Board too small: 2 members

score_details.score_summary: National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics shows moderate alignment and limited impact (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: No explicit mention of zakat donations found for the National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics in the provided search results. The search results describe NAMCC as a non-profit, 501(c)(3) organization that unites healthcare organizations and clinics, and mentions fundraising sources and grant opportunities, but does not specifically state that they accept zakat. (Source: https://naomcc.org)

all_citations.claim: The organization is a network that unites healthcare organizations and provides support for member clinics.
Financial data including total revenue and program expense ratios are currently unavailable or not reported.
The organization accepts general charitable donations (sadaqah) to support its mission of assisting member clinics.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a lower overall score due to significant gaps in financial transparency and a lack of verifiable impact data. While it fills a niche in healthcare networking, the absence of public financial filings limits donor confidence.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public disclosure of financial metrics and program outcomes
Lack of third-party evaluations regarding cost-effectiveness and impact

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the organization addresses the urgent cause of global health, it currently shows low alignment for many donors due to a lack of detailed reporting and a limited track record of transparent operations [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization's impact is categorized under capacity building, as it focuses on supporting other clinics rather than direct patient care [1]. However, there is no available data regarding the cost per beneficiary or specific evidence of program effectiveness [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics serves as a unifying network for healthcare organizations dedicated to providing charitable medical services.
strengths: Serves as a specialized network for Muslim-led charitable medical providers
Focuses on capacity building and resource sharing among member organizations
baseline_narrative.summary: The National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics (NAOMCC) operates as a representative body that unites various healthcare organizations to streamline charitable medical efforts [1]. The organization focuses on providing essential support and resources to its member clinics to enhance their operational capacity [3].
